reasoning: The rule requires that ClientHello, EndOfEarlyData, ServerHello, Finished, and KeyUpdate messages MUST be sent aligned with a record boundary. The code enforces this on the receiving side by checking RECORD_LAYER_processed_read_pending() which detects if more data exists in the current record after the message (i.e., the message did NOT end at a record boundary). If pending data is found, the code issues SSL_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE and aborts. (1) KeyUpdate (statem_lib.c:706-709): unconditional check since KeyUpdate is TLS 1.3-only. (2) Finished (statem_lib.c:855-860): check guarded by SSL_CONNECTION_IS_TLS13(s), correct since record boundary requirement only applies in TLS 1.3. (3) ServerHello (statem_clnt.c:1966-1972): check inside SSL_CONNECTION_IS_TLS13(s) block. (4) EndOfEarlyData (statem_srvr.c:4735-4738): unconditional check since EndOfEarlyData is TLS 1.3-only. (5) ClientHello (statem_srvr.c:1904-1908): check guarded by SSL_CONNECTION_IS_TLS13(s). All five message types have the record boundary check implemented and all violations result in SSL_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE.

evidence.expected_behavior: KeyUpdate, Finished, ServerHello, ClientHello, and EndOfEarlyData messages must end at a record boundary; if they do not, the implementation must abort with an unexpected_message alert.
